lanes.purpose: Quick local sanity before or during a small task.
lanes.when_to_run: After small edits that could touch layering or before handing off a partial branch.
lanes.notes: Fast is not enough for final completion of runtime, eval, or operations tasks.

lanes.purpose: Normal Codex completion checks for most Python/docs/governance tasks.
lanes.when_to_run: Before final response for most tasks unless a narrower user request explicitly scopes verification down.
Before origin sync for large repo-wide tasks.
lanes.notes: Does not include optional Cargo checks.

lanes.purpose: Docs, index, README, audit, or command metadata edits.
lanes.when_to_run: After documentation-only changes that do not touch executable Python paths.
lanes.notes: Use standard/full if docs update accepted status, eval posture, public-alpha safety, or parity claims.

lanes.when_to_run: After web/API route-policy, status, route inventory, or hosting-pack changes.
lanes.notes: This is rehearsal evidence only and not production readiness.

lanes.purpose: Python-oracle and future Rust parity discipline.
lanes.when_to_run: After Python oracle, source-registry, query-planner, local-index, memory, or Rust parity fixture changes.
lanes.notes: Rust candidate commands live in rust_optional until Cargo is required.

lanes.when_to_run: After eval runner, eval fixture, search usefulness, planner, index, or source-coverage changes.
lanes.notes: External baseline observations remain manual and pending unless committed as evidence records.

lanes.purpose: Optional Rust skeleton and candidate seam checks.
lanes.when_to_run: Only when Cargo is installed in the local environment.
lanes.notes: Never fail normal Python verification solely because Cargo is unavailable.
